person.bio: I’m a freelance platform engineer specializing in Kubernetes, Terraform, and CI/CD automation, with strong experience across Azure and multi-cloud environments. I focus on building efficient, scalable systems that bridge data and infrastructure, and I regularly share knowledge at meetups and online.

person.name: Yoshiaki Sakota
person.bio: I work as an SRE at TOKYO GAS Co., Ltd. Over the past several years, I've been involved in system development using a variety of CNCF technologies, particularly focusing on Kubernetes and Argo CD. I'm passionate about leveraging these technologies to deliver agile and valuable services to our customers.

person.name: Buglioni Thomas
person.bio: I am a Full Stack DevSecOps engineer at Orange, specializing in Prometheus, Linux, Kubernetes, Python, Terraform, and Ansible. Passionate about continuous learning and improvement, I focus on automating and securing cloud-native infrastructures while implementing robust CI/CD and security practices. I strive to optimize both team performance and system efficiency to deliver scalable and secure solutions.

person.name: Chris Mazur
person.bio: I'm a software/devops engineer with a strong vocation for tech communities. I founded a few conferences including Cloud Native Summit / Service Mesh Day / ScaleConf NZ / FullStack Day / PHP New Zealand / Connecta.dev / Auckland Cloud Native Meetup, Wellington Devops Meetup and more... and I Co-Founded PHP Conference Argentina. My goal is helping to connect the Software Development Community in the Southern Hemisphere. Originally from Argentina, my family and I moved to New Zealand more than 10 years ago, where we still live today, in the beautiful city of Wellington.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: First of all, receiving recognition from the CNCF is an honour and a great support to continue working for the local tech community. It is definitely a great opportunity to open new doors and offer more opportunities to our community and I am very much looking forward to working with many other colleagues and friends in this ambassador program. Thanks!

person.name: Joshua Taylor
person.bio: Experienced Devops Engineer working with Kubernetes, Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD, and various cloud deployments throughout AWS, GCP, & Azure. I enjoy working on projects in Go and Rust. Outside of work, I love learning C++ and C# for video game development in Unreal Engine and Unity.

person.name: Munirathnakumar Sivaramakrishnan
person.bio: With 17 years of IT experience in the financial services sector, I specialize in assessing and ensuring the security of cloud and Kubernetes platforms. My expertise spans Cloud Security, Container Security, Kubernetes Security, SRE, DevOps, and Production Engineering. I focus on hardening infrastructures to protect sensitive data and meet stringent compliance standards.

person.name: Alicio Cerqueira
person.bio: Seasoned Senior DevOps/SRE Engineer with over 10 years designing, building, and scaling mission-critical payment/financial platforms and massive high-scale entertainment platforms using Kubernetes, GitOps, and advanced Containerization. Passionate about DevOps culture and platform engineering, helping companies evolve infrastructure by creating secure, resilient Kubernetes environments. Continuously expanding my CNCF expertise through technology evaluation and production adoption.

person.name: Anudeep Nalla
person.bio: Anudeep Nalla is a Kubernetes Security Specialist and Platform Engineer with expertise in CNCF technologies like Kubernetes, Kyverno, Rook-Ceph, and Rancher. He holds multiple advanced certifications (CKS, CKA, CKAD, RHCA Level III). Outside of tech, Anudeep is a USPA B-licensed skydiver—showcasing his commitment to both adventure and discipline—and balances his engineering life with a passion for fitness and trekking

person.name: Kate Qiu
person.bio:
Kate is an Event Planner at The Linux Foundation, helping with the fulfillment of sponsorship benefits for Kubecon + CloudNativeCon events.
Prior to her role at LF, Kate worked as a Marketing Operations Assistant for China Telecom Americas where she was responsible for their industry conference and tradeshow planning and logistics.

person.name: Khin Sandar Soe
person.bio: I am a passionate cloud and DevOps with hands-on experience in Kubernetes and CNCF technologies. I am currently working toward becoming a Kubestronaut and continuously improving my skills in cloud engineering. I enjoy sharing knowledge through community learning and technical discussions, and I actively explore tools and practices that improve system reliability and scalability. I am committed to continuous growth and building impactful solutions in the cloud-native ecosystem.

person.name: Matthew Rodgers
person.bio: I am a Senior Software Engineer with a passion for cloud-native technologies and the Kubernetes ecosystem. I have experience developing modern applications, migrating legacy systems, and deploying tools that enhance workflows within Kubernetes environments. I enjoy solving complex challenges around scalability and reliability while writing clean, maintainable code. Excited to keep learning, I look forward to deepening my expertise and contributing to the Kubernetes community.

person.name: Yoshiyuki Tabata
person.bio: Yoshiyuki Tabata is a Senior OSS Consultant at Hitachi, Ltd. As an expert in Identity and Access Management (IAM) and APIs, he has provided numerous consultations over the past decade, including designing API and Authentication/Authorization platforms. He has actively contributed to CNCF TAG Security by facilitating APAC region community calls and leading the IAM white paper project, and he has added significant functionalities to OSS projects such as Keycloak. As a CNCF Ambassador, he has delivered talks at events like KubeCon, Apidays, and Open Source Summit focusing on secure solutions for the CNCF ecosystem. Additionally, as a Cloud Native Community Japan organizer and a Cloud Native Security Japan founder, he has organized several CNCF meetups in Japan.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I aim to improve the adoption and security of CNCF technologies in Japan by sharing my expertise from numerous projects. As a CNCF ambassador, I want to disseminate solutions and best practices for a more secure CNCF ecosystem.

person.name: Lisa-Marie Namphy
person.bio: Lisa is a developer advocate and community architect, and is Head of Developer Relations at Intuit (home of Argo Proj). Lisa is also a CNCF Ambassador and Silicon Valley Cloud Native User Group organizer (with over 7000 members). Lisa is an advocate and frequent speaker for open source technology and Diversity, Equity & Inclusion initiatives, a writer, an avid sports fan, and loves wine and dogs. Follow Lisa on Twitter @SWDevAngel and LinkedIn @lnamphy.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: It has been a great pleasure to be a CNCF Ambassador for the past several years and I am thrilled to continue the journey. I love working with this particular group of colleagues to evangelize open source and cloud native technology, to mentor new community members, to give a platform and a voice and to encourage participation in ALL areas of the cloud native ecosystem. And to have the support of the CNCF to extend the reach of cloud native technology to "the last mile" has been an extremely rewarding experience that I am grateful and excited to continue! :) Looking so much forward to working with all of you in the coming years! :)

person.name: Michał Nyga
person.bio: Michał is a DevOps Engineer, specializing in internal development platforms based on Kubernetes clusters. In addition, he shares his knowledge with others, guided by the motto - ”be the senior you needed when you were a junior”.

person.name: Yanick Egli
person.bio: I'm a driven and skilled cloud-native enthusiast specializing in Kubernetes Security and Public Cloud Kubernetes + Toolchain as a Service offerings. Constantly evolving and deepening my expertise, I thrive in exploring and leveraging new CNCF projects.  Passionate about staying ahead in the cloud-native space and giving back to the community.

person.name: José Ureba Camacho
person.bio: Driven by a naturally curious mind, I am a cloud-native enthusiast specializing in CNCF technologies, with expertise in Kubernetes, observability, and security. I am dedicated to building secure and scalable cloud-native applications and infrastructure, with a strong commitment to DevOps practices and seamless automation. Beyond technology, I have a deep passion for music, cinema, and literature.

person.name: Radoslav Rachev
person.bio: I’m in the IT sector more than 13 years. During these years I moved through different positions and technologies. I started as Backup support engineer then moved to the DevOps field where I learn lost of different technologies and practices. I am currently working as a Cloud Consultant mainly focused on AWS and their services. I’m person who is open to any new technologies and strives to learn something new every day, as it contributes to my personal and professional development. I like to move myself out of my comfort zone, continuously seeking challenges that push my boundaries and foster growth. In my free time I  enjoy being with my family, reading books, hiking and swimming

person.name: Sergio Turrent
person.bio: I'm an embedded engineer working with Azure Kubernetes services. Opensource and GNU/Linux enthusiast that also enjoys playing music (live looping with multiple instruments) and coffee aficionado.

person.name: Sorin-Marius Popa
person.bio: I started my career as a QA Automation Engineer, where I was first exposed to cloud technologies. Shortly after, I discovered Kubernetes, which quickly sparked my interest in containerization and cloud-native ecosystems. My initial goal was to achieve the Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) certification, which ultimately helped me transition into a DevOps Engineer role. After learning about the Kubestronaut Program, I became motivated to take on the challenge and have thoroughly enjoyed the journey ever since.

person.name: Vishwa Nageshwar Kodumagulla
person.bio: A results-driven DevOps & SRE professional with expertise in cloud computing, CI/CD automation, Kubernetes operations, infrastructure as code, and end-to-end monitoring. Passionate about building resilient infrastructure and optimizing production system reliability.

person.name: Robert Volkmann
person.bio: As an experienced and passionate Cloud Native Architect, I accelerate my clients' Private Cloud journeys by designing and implementing customized, high-performance, and secure on-premise bare-metal Kubernetes platforms. I leverage my technical expertise in CNCF technologies to guide cross-functional teams in orchestrating implementation strategies, optimizing system performance, and driving scalable cloud-native solutions. Additionally, I promote the long-term success and sustainability of the infrastructure by providing tailored training for employees.

person.name: Sipathamandla Shoko
person.bio: Platform Engineering Lead building and maintaining multi-tenant Kubernetes based platforms. GitOps using FluxCD, IaC using OpenTofu. Working on Golang based Operators.

person.name: Kaung Htet Aung
person.bio: Software engineer specializing in cloud infrastructure, Kubernetes, and secure system design. Experienced in building scalable systems and solving real-world engineering challenges through practical solutions. Strong background in leading technical initiatives, guiding teams, and delivering reliable cloud-native platforms. Passionate about continuous learning and contributing to the cloud-native ecosystem.

person.name: Ken Takagi
person.bio: I work as an SRE at Oisix ra Daichi Inc., an e-commerce company, where I am dedicated to enhancing the reliability of our products. I am committed to delivering robust and scalable services using cloud-native technologies, with a strong focus on Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) and Terraform. I am also passionate about applying SRE best practices to drive operational efficiency, infrastructure improvements, and enhanced observability.

person.name: Kārlis Akots Gribulis
person.bio: I am a Latvian container platform engineer with focus on building, maintaining and improving Kubernetes platforms and developer experience within them. Actively use and promote open source products within my workplaces. Have also made some minor open source contributions to products, such as, Grafana, Prometheus, etc.

person.name: Mathijs Hoogland
person.bio: I'm a freelance DevOps engineer with a specialisation in Kubernetes and cloud native environments. I love helping companies transform technically and culturally to fully grasp the advantages of Kubernetes. Usually the tech is easy, it's about people.
